compiled_state.title: Planning pipeline: external-dependency classification and manual-step handshake
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context

When an epic's success criteria include outcomes observable only in deployed environments, external services, or other resources outside the codebase, our planning pipeline (brainstorm -> preplanning -> implementation-plan -> sprint) has no mechanism to distinguish them from pure-code criteria or to plan for the manual steps needed to make them true. This caused epic 241f-ce0d in a host project to ship code-complete while SC8 remained blocked on an analyst dogfooding session that required a staging environment no one had planned to provision — because the planning skills never asked. This epic closes that gap by making external dependencies a first-class planning artifact and giving sprint a principled way to hand off manual steps to the practitioner.

## Success Criteria

1. /dso:brainstorm Phase 1 scans draft success criteria with a shape heuristic — criteria describing outcomes observable in deployed/external contexts (vs. pure code) — and when any criterion matches, runs an External Dependencies classification dialogue and renders a distinct ## External Dependencies block in the epic description. Each block entry carries required fields: name, ownership (exists / this-epic / linked-epic-<id>), handling (claude_auto / user_manual), claude_has_access (yes / no / unknown), and verification_command (optional with required justification if omitted). [Observable: epic description rendered by brainstorm contains the section when heuristic fires.]

2. /dso:brainstorm blocks the Phase 2 approval gate on unresolved contradictions. When a dependency is declared handling=claude_auto + claude_has_access=no, the gate refuses to present until the practitioner resolves it (typically by flipping handling to user_manual). When verification_command is omitted, sprint's pause handshake requires the user to type a confirmation token and logs it to the ticket as an audit trail. [Observable: approval gate refuses on unresolved contradiction; audit comment present on tickets with verification-less manual stories.]

3. /dso:preplanning reads the External Dependencies block and emits correctly-shaped stories. handling=claude_auto entries generate automation stories; handling=user_manual entries generate stories tagged manual:awaiting_user whose done definition contains the instructions + verification_command (or confirmation-token prompt) from the block. Preplanning refuses to decompose any externally-shaped SC when the block is missing or incomplete. [Observable: decomposition output contains correctly-tagged stories matching the block; refusal diagnostic present when block missing.]

4. /dso:implementation-plan guards against manual-tagged stories. When invoked on a manual:awaiting_user-tagged story, the skill branches: if no prep work is needed (no verification script to author, no user-facing instructions to draft, no CLI wrapper to add), it refuses decomposition with a clear diagnostic; if prep work IS needed, it decomposes ONLY the prep tasks using standard RED/GREEN/UPDATE classification and reads the parent epic's External Dependencies block to seed prep-task context. The manual verification step itself never appears as a task. [Observable: implementation-plan produces prep-only task lists or principled refusals on manual stories; never hallucinates RED tasks for manual verification.]

5. /dso:sprint drains autonomous work first, then pauses in-session on manual stories. Stories tagged manual:awaiting_user are filtered from the autonomous batch. When only manual-tagged stories remain, sprint presents a single blocking prompt listing per-story instructions + verification_command. Accepts done, done <story-id>, and skip; on done with a verification_command present, sprint runs it and proceeds on pass or re-prompts with failure output on fail; on done with no verification_command, sprint requires a confirmation token and logs it. Topological sort surfaces manual stories before any dependents so autonomous work is not blocked on a user who has not been prompted yet. [Observable: sprint session on a mixed epic reaches the manual pause after autonomous work, handles each input variant correctly, and resumes.]

6. The entire feature sits behind a config flag planning.external_dependency_block_enabled (default off initially). When off, all four skills behave identically to current baseline. When on, the new behaviors activate end-to-end across brainstorm / preplanning / implementation-plan / sprint with no partial-activation states. [Observable: flag toggle switches behavior end-to-end; existing epics created before flag-on continue to sprint without regression.]

7. A regression test reproduces the SC8-class failure shape. An epic draft with at least one deployed/external-shaped success criterion but no External Dependencies block fails the brainstorm -> preplanning pipeline after this epic ships (specifically: preplanning refuses per SC3). [Observable: regression test in place and green.]

## Dependencies

None.

## Approach

Config-gated, additive across four skills. Brainstorm: shape-heuristic scan in Phase 1 + classification dialogue + block renderer + contradiction gate + audit-token handling for verification-less entries. Preplanning: block reader + tagged-story generator (manual:awaiting_user) + refusal diagnostic when block missing. Implementation-plan: tag-detection guard + prep-only decomposition branch + block reader for task context. Sprint: tag filter for autonomous drain + blocking manual-pause handshake with done/skip/done <story-id> input handling, verification_command execution, confirmation-token logging, and topological-sort adjustment.

## Scenario Analysis

Inline red-team pass surfaced three issues, folded into the spec above:
- Verification-less manual stories have no catch on false "done" claims -> SC2 confirmation-token + audit comment requirement.
- manual:awaiting_user blocking a dependent autonomous story -> SC5 topological-sort clause ensuring manual stories surface before their dependents drain.
- Brainstorm re-invoke on an epic with existing External Dependencies block -> addressed by standard idempotent upsert in preplanning's existing re-invocation guard; not a new SC.

## Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered — bright-line conditions none (external integration mentioned is target-domain context, not implementation surface).
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered via inline compact pass — scenarios surviving filter: 2 of 7 (rolled into SC2 and SC5); 1 additional rolled into approach guidance.
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none.
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered.
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered.
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — this epic modifies four SKILL.md files; structural-boundary testing standard applies (behavioral-testing-standard.md rule 5).

## Provenance

Dialogue source: /dso:brainstorm session on 2026-04-17 seeded from a host-project planning-gap report (epic 241f-ce0d SC8 analyst dogfooding blocked on un-provisioned staging). Two process bugs filed during the session: b784-fe6e (two-questions-at-once) and f9a4-24e2 (unresolved scope in Understanding Summary).

comments.body: Adversarial review: 10 findings from red team, 9 accepted (1 rejected: complexity-evaluator timing mismatch). Surviving findings applied inline during story drafting (no post-hoc ticket edits needed). Blue team artifact: /tmp/workflow-plugin-f3cd706b951bc369/adversarial-review-3d19-ea1d.json

comments.body:
### Planning Intelligence Log

(Brainstorm streamlined pass — original PIL content preserved in description's "## Planning Intelligence Log" section. This comment added with H3 marker to satisfy brainstorm:complete tag validation.)

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered — bright-line conditions none (external integration mentioned is target-domain context, not implementation surface).
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered via inline compact pass — scenarios surviving filter: 2 of 7 (rolled into SC2 and SC5); 1 additional rolled into approach guidance.
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none.
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered.
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered.
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — this epic modifies four SKILL.md files; structural-boundary testing standard applies (behavioral-testing-standard.md rule 5).
- Scale context (Step 0): small scale (default) — per-planning-session, per-sprint; not volume-sensitive.
